title: New York Philharmonic gives its first concert
note: 1842; current is a modern Avery Fisher Hall photo. No reliable period image (only an ambiguous 'Copy of hill.jpg') found on Commons.

title: Metropolitan Opera House opens on Broadway
note: 1883; current is the 1905 period photo of the 39th Street Met — genuine period image.

title: Tin Pan Alley makes 28th Street the sound of America
note: 1893; current Tinpanalley.jpg is a genuine period photograph of the block.

title: First Macy's Thanksgiving parade steps off
commonsFile: File:Felix the Cat at 1927 Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade 1.png
note: Current image is Detroit's 'America's Thanksgiving Parade 2024' — wrong city and modern. Replaced with a 1927 Macy's parade photo (Felix the Cat balloon).

title: "Show Boat" premieres and reinvents the musical
commonsFile: File:Sammy White-Eva Puck in Show Boat (1927).jpg
note: Current shows director James Whale (wrong subject). Replaced with a 1927 production photo of the original Broadway Show Boat cast.

title: Whitney Museum of American Art opens
note: Current is a modern photo of the 2015 Meatpacking building. Replaced with a c.1909 period portrait of founder Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney.

title: Apollo Theater opens in Harlem
note: 1934; current is a modern marquee photo. No period image of the 1934 Apollo on Commons (only modern shots and a 1915 pre-Apollo bill).

title: First Tony Awards honor Broadway
note: 1947; current is a modern photo of a Tony medallion (a design postdating 1947). No period 1947 ceremony image available.

title: Frank Lloyd Wright's Guggenheim opens
note: 1959; current is a genuine period portrait of Frank Lloyd Wright, who died months before the opening.

title: Free Shakespeare opens the Delacorte Theater
note: 1962; current is a 2021 photo. No 1962-era Commons image of the Delacorte's first season found.

title: Lincoln Center opens with Philharmonic Hall
note: 1962; current is a modern David Geffen Hall photo. No period 1962 opening image on Commons.

title: Chinese Exclusion Act freezes Chinatown's growth
note: Current image is an Australia–China locator map (wrong subject). Replaced with a period image of the 1882 Chinese Exclusion Act itself.

title: The Great Migration remakes Harlem
note: 1916; current is a c.1900 census map of Black population share — a period map, an allowed category and thematically on point.

title: First Feast of San Gennaro on Mulberry Street
note: 1926; current is a historical devotional painting of Saint Januarius (San Gennaro), not a modern photo — period-appropriate.

title: The Mets play their first home game
note: 1962; current is the era-correct 1962–1992 Mets insignia — contemporaneous with the event.

title: The current Madison Square Garden opens
commonsFile: File:Billy Graham at Madison Square Garden 1969 (cropped).jpg
note: 1968; current is a modern MSG exterior. Replaced with a 1969 photo inside the newly opened fourth Garden.

title: West Indian Day Parade moves to Eastern Parkway
note: 1969; current is a 2008 parade photo. No 1969-era Commons image available.

title: The Miracle Mets win the World Series
note: 1969; current is the present-day Mets logo. Replaced with the era-correct 1962–1992 insignia (the logo in use in 1969).

title: Bellevue begins, the oldest US public hospital
note: Current is a modern photo of Bellevue's atrium. Replaced with a period NYPL image of the almshouse hospital where Bellevue began.

title: First passenger elevator opens on Broadway
note: 1857; current is a period image of the E. V. Haughwout Building on Broadway — the actual site.

title: City fetes the transatlantic telegraph cable
commonsFile: File:41 William England - Atlantic telegraph jubilee on Broadway, New York.jpg
note: Current is a 1918 Brooklyn Navy Yard photo (wrong subject/era). Replaced with an 1858 period view of the Atlantic telegraph jubilee on Broadway.

title: Western Union tower rises over Broadway
note: 1875; current is a period image of the Western Union Telegraph Building — genuine period depiction.

title: New York Stock Exchange opens its Broad St. home
commonsFile: File:New York Stock Exchange, Broad Street LCCN00650325.jpg
note: Current is a modern panoramio photo. Replaced with an early-1900s Library of Congress photo of the Broad Street NYSE building.

title: Federal Reserve Bank of New York opens
note: Current is the bank's modern logo. Replaced with a period Library of Congress portrait of Benjamin Strong Jr., who opened the bank in 1914.

title: Polio epidemic paralyzes the city
note: 1916; current is a genuine 1916 newspaper front page on the polio epidemic — contemporaneous.

title: Air conditioning cools the Rivoli Theatre
note: Current is a 2016 photo of the WTC Oculus (wrong subject entirely). Replaced with a 1923 period image of the Rivoli Theatre.

title: Nuyorican Poets Cafe is born
note: 1973; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: CBGB opens on the Bowery
note: 1973; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: Def Jam is founded in an NYU dorm
note: 1984; post-1970, contemporaneous label logo acceptable.

title: Ali-Frazier 'Fight of the Century' at the Garden
note: 1971; post-1970, contemporaneous fight poster acceptable.

title: Soviet Jews make Brighton Beach 'Little Odessa'
note: 1975; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: US Open moves to Flushing Meadows
note: 1978; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: Tenement Museum founded at 97 Orchard Street
note: 1988; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: A new immigrant wave remakes the city
note: 1990; post-1970, contemporaneous demographic graphic acceptable.

title: ACT UP forms to fight the AIDS crisis
note: 1987; post-1970, contemporaneous logo acceptable.

title: "Silicon Alley" names the city's dot-com boom
note: c.1995; post-1970, contemporaneous Flatiron photo acceptable.

title: Fresh Kills recovery operation ends
note: 2002; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.

title: OMNY brings tap-to-pay to the subway
note: 2019; post-1970, contemporaneous photo acceptable.
